Admission
How to Apply
Inquire
Visit
Apply
Apply today!
Take the 1st Step: Complete the Inquiry Form
The Admission Team is here to guide you throughout the application process.
How to Apply
The application deadline for the Fall 2026-2027 school year is
January 15, 2026
. Admission decisions will be available on March 10, 2026, in the applicant portal for applicants who submit the application by the January 15 deadline.
Inquiry Form:
Start here so we can keep you informed through the admission process
Interview:
Schedule your
interview by December 19, 2025
using the following link:
Schedule Here
Interviews may be conducted in-person or virtually and are strongly recommended.
Application:
Submit your application through
Gateway to Prep Schools
.
Teacher Recommendations:
Completed through the Gateway application portal.
School Transcripts:
Official academic records submitted by the applicant's current school for the prior academic year and first semester of the current academic year.
(Optional) Apply for
Financial Aid
by
January 15, 2026
.
(Optional) Complete the
Character Skills
Snapshot
.
English Proficiency Test:
Strongly recommended for all international applicants. Required if the applicant has
not
attended a school in a country where English is the official language for the past two years. Webb accepts TOEFL, IELTS, or Duolingo.
Webb is test-blind and does not consider standardized tests like the SSAT (Secondary School Admission Test) or the Independent School Entrance Exam (ISEE) in its application process. The Admission Team evaluates each applicant holistically, considering their academic performance, teacher recommendations, and personal experiences. Our approach is designed to identify potential and excellence in future Webb students, recognizing the unique strengths and qualities each individual brings.
If you have any questions about the application process, please get in touch with us at
admission@webb.org
or (909) 482-5214.
Non-Discrimination Policy
Webb admits students of any race, color, national and ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, or any other characteristic protected by state or federal law to all rights, privileges, programs, and activities generally accorded or made available to students at the schools. Webb does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by state or federal law in the administration of their educational policies, tuition assistance, athletic, and other school-administered programs, or any other basis in law.

NOTICE OF NONDISCRIMINATORY POLICY AS TO STUDENTS.
The Webb School admits students of any race, color, national and ethnic origin to all the rights, privileges, programs, and activities generally accorded or made available to students at the school. It does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national and ethnic origin in administration of its educational policies, admissions policies, scholarship and loan programs, and athletic and other school-administered programs.
